evals.prompt: Create a PRD for adding saved searches with email alerts to this application. Users should be able to save a search, choose how often they want updates, and manage existing alerts from their account area.
evals.expected_output: A GitHub issue containing a PRD that explores existing search, account, notification, and test patterns before asking product-scope questions and filing the issue.
expectations: The workflow launches multiple code-explorer subagents with distinct lenses before writing the PRD.
The workflow performs targeted research from official sources before recording implementation decisions.
The workflow asks one user question at a time and includes a recommended answer for each question.
The final PRD follows the required template and includes a GitHub issue title/body plus the intended gh issue create command for offline filing.
Implementation decisions cite durable codebase patterns or official research links without including specific file paths.

evals.prompt: I need a product requirements doc filed as an issue for a team admin audit log. Admins need to see who changed user roles, billing settings, and SSO configuration, with enough detail for compliance review.
evals.expected_output: A PRD issue for an admin audit log that investigates existing admin, billing, auth/SSO, compliance, and testing patterns, resolves scope and retention questions, and cites evidence for decisions.
expectations: The workflow gathers codebase context through parallel code-explorer agents and asks each for 5-10 key files with reasons.
The workflow identifies security, retention, permissions, and compliance gaps and interviews the user one question at a time.
The workflow researches official security, privacy, platform, or framework guidance before recommending decisions.
The PRD contains an extensive numbered user-story list covering primary, edge, admin, compliance, and error-state scenarios.
Testing decisions focus on external behavior and cite similar codebase test patterns where available.

evals.prompt: Draft and open a PRD issue for improving onboarding: when a new workspace is created, guide the user through inviting teammates, connecting integrations, and completing the first useful setup action.
evals.expected_output: A PRD GitHub issue for onboarding improvements that uses codebase exploration, official UX/accessibility research, and a dependency-aware interview before filing.
expectations: The workflow distinguishes product-scope questions from questions answerable by exploration or official documentation.
The workflow walks dependent decisions in order, resolving parent decisions before child decisions.
The PRD avoids specific file paths and code snippets while still citing existing product or architectural patterns.
The Out of Scope section clearly excludes deferred onboarding paths, integrations, or implementation work.
The final output includes the GitHub issue title/body, intended gh issue create command, and any important assumptions or follow-up notes.
